title: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents
notes: Defines the profiling-memory-planning-action decomposition used as a baseline architecture.

title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
notes: Canonical memory stream plus reflection architecture for believable long-lived agents.

title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
notes: Useful for core memory, archival memory, and agent-managed memory primitives.

title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory
notes: Production memory layer candidate; useful for comparing custom memory governance versus managed memory.

title: A Survey on the Memory Mechanism of Large Language Model based Agents
notes: Memory taxonomy and prior art map for agent memory systems.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
notes: Basic reasoning-action loop that many tool-using agents build on.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
notes: Important for self-critique, reflection, and episodic feedback loops.

title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
notes: Planning and deliberation baseline; useful but not sufficient for will.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
notes: Key reference for agents escaping pure chat through environment action and skill accumulation.

title: Inner Monologue: Embodied Reasoning through Planning with Language Models
Proactive Agent: Shifting LLM Agents from Reactive Responses to Active Assistance
notes: Directly relevant to proactive behavior as an observable surface of will.

title: Long-term Task-Oriented Agent
notes: Candidate reference for intent-conditioned monitoring and event-triggered follow-up; verify details.

title: PASK: A Proactive Agent with Self-Evolving Knowledge
notes: Needs verification; useful if source exists and matches proactive memory claims.

title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage
notes: Important for defining why will requires externalized goals, checkpoints, and anti-drift controls.

title: Homeostatic Reinforcement Learning for Integrating Reward Collection and Physiological Stability
notes: Foundational bridge from internal needs to action selection.

title: Continuous Homeostatic Reinforcement Learning
notes: Useful for modeling internal state variables and drive-like behavior.

title: The Basic AI Drives
notes: Required safety reference once yizhi discusses self-maintenance, resources, and reproduction.
